servo_attach.tooltip: Initialize the servo of the specified pin. ESP32 recommends using GPIO 4,5,12-19,21-23,25-27,32,33. Avoid using GPIO 6-11 (flash) and 34-39 (input only)

servo_attach_advanced.message0:
Initialization pin %1 servo
Minimum pulse width %2 Maximum pulse width %3
servo_attach_advanced.tooltip: Initialize the servo of the specified pin and set the pulse width range. ESP32 will automatically assign PWM channels, no need to manually specify

servo_attach_full.message0:
Initialization pin %1 servo
Minimum angle %2 Maximum angle %3
Minimum pulse width %4 Maximum pulse width %5
servo_attach_full.tooltip: Initialize the servo of the specified pin and set the complete parameters (angle range, pulse width range). ESP32 will automatically assign PWM channels

servo_write.message0: Control pin %1 Servo rotation angle %2 degrees
servo_write.tooltip: Set the target angle of the specified pin servo (0-180 degrees)

servo_write_float.message0: Control pin %1 Servo rotation angle %2 degrees
servo_write_float.tooltip: Set the precise angle of the specified pin servo (supports floating point numbers)

servo_write_microseconds.message0: Control pin %1 servo setting pulse width is %2 microseconds
servo_write_microseconds.tooltip: Directly set the pulse width value of the specified pin servo (usually 544-2400 microseconds)

servo_read.message0: Read pin %1 current angle of servo
servo_read.tooltip: Read the current angle value of the specified pin servo

servo_read_microseconds.message0: Read pin %1 current pulse width of servo
servo_read_microseconds.tooltip: Read the current pulse width value of the specified pin servo (microseconds)

servo_attached.tooltip: Check whether the servo of the specified pin is connected

servo_detach.tooltip: Disconnect the specified pin servo from the pin

servo_get_pin.message0: Pin %1 The pin number of the servo connection
servo_get_pin.tooltip: Get the pin number of the specified servo connection (return the input pin number)

servo_map_angle.message0:
Maps value %1 from the range %2 to %3
to angle range %4 to %5
servo_map_angle.tooltip: Map a range of values to the servo angle range

servo_sweep.message0:
Pin %1 Servo scan
Delay from %2 degrees to %3 degrees Delay %4 milliseconds
servo_sweep.tooltip: Let the servo of the specified pin scan within the specified angle range
